title: Algorithms 1: Searching and Sorting
description: Linear and binary search, and the bubble, insertion and merge sort algorithms, traced step-by-step on small example lists.
objectives: Trace linear search and binary search on a small list and count the comparisons each makes
Explain why binary search requires the input data to be sorted, and state its comparison advantage over linear search
Trace bubble sort and insertion sort pass-by-pass on a small list
Trace merge sort's divide-and-conquer splitting and the merge step on a small list
State the informal best/worst-case behaviour of each algorithm and choose an appropriate one for a given situation
